data.title: Dynex Capital GAAP EPS of -$0.41, net interest income of $79.25M
data.description:
• Dynex Capital press release (DX): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.41.
  • Net interest income of $79.25M (+82.3% Y/Y).

data.title: Tesla adds Dallas and Houston to the robotaxi rollout push
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) announced over the weekend that it is now offering robotaxi rides in Dallas and Houston. Within Texas, the company has already been running a geofenced self-driving taxi service network in Austin since last year.

The Elon Musk-led company posted a video on its official X account that showed a driverless Model Y without a safety monitor in the passenger seat. Tesla (TSLA) also highlighted the robotaxi service areas for both Houston and Dallas.

 

 

In addition to the three Texas cities, Tesla (TSLA) has a robotaxi service in the San Francisco Bay area, although it still deploys a safety monitor. The company has pointed to planned robotaxi expansion in Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas. Investors are likely to hear much more about Tesla's (TSLA) robotaxi plans during the earnings conference call on Wednesday at 4:30 p.m.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) edged 0.8% lower in premarket trading to $397.69 vs. the 52-week range of $222.79 to $498.83.

data.title: Transoceanic Investments updates OTCQB ticker to 'OCEEF' following name change
Marvell gains, Broadcom, Celestica dip on Google report
data.description:
Marvell Technology (MRVL) gained more than 6% in premarket trading on Monday after it was reported that the specialty semiconductor company may work with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) on developing future artificial intelligence chips.

Conversely, existing Google partners, Broadcom (AVGO) and Celestica (CLS), both declined 1.5% and 1%, respectively, on the report.

On Sunday, The Information reported that Marvell was talking to Google about developing two new chips aimed at improving the efficiency of running AI models. One of the chips, according to the report, would be a memory processing unit intended to work alongside Google’s tensor processing unit, or TPU. The second would be a new TPU specifically engineered for AI inference workloads.

Google and Marvell did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Wells Fargo said a deal for Google's chip business would be a “meaningful incremental positive” for Marvell and provide “increased confidence” in its ability to diversify customers. Marvell's major customers for its custom XPU business are Amazon (AMZN) and Microsoft (MSFT).

“[We] think the noted Memory Processing Unit win could reflect MRVL's Structera CXL solutions,” analyst Aaron Rakers wrote in a note to clients. Rakers has an Overweight rating and a $135 price target on Marvell.

“During our meetings, CEO Matt Murphy had highlighted that it has 3 hyperscaler design wins for the co's Structera CXL solutions - focused on leveraging CXL pooling of existing memory capacity across their compute infra,” Rakers added. “At OFC in mid-March, Marvell introduced their Structera S 30260 - a new 260-lane CXL switch to enable rack-level memory pooling working in conjunction w/ the company's Structera A near-memory accelerators, Structera X memory-expansion controllers, and Alaska PCIe/CXL retimers.”

data.title: BNB Plus stock jumps after starting strategic review
data.description:
BNB Plus (BNBX) stock surged 9.4% in Monday premarket trading after the digital asset treasury company  authorized a formal review of strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value, the company said Monday.

In the past six months, BNB Plus stock has plunged 83%.

The company, which is currently focused on accumulating Binance's BNB token (BNB-USD), is initially focusing on market sectors that it believes have potential for high growth, including artificial intelligence infrastructure, digital infrastructure, critical domestic raw materials, defense technologies, and healthcare, among others.

BNB Plus (BNBX) is considering such actions as a merger, reverse merger, business combination, acquisition, asset sale, joint venture, and capital-raising transactions, among others.

The company's treasury, as of 1 PM ET on April 18, 2026, had a value of ~$12.2M, representing 3.4x its current market capitalization.

The firm has retained Lucid Capital Markets as its financial advisor to assist in the review process. It has provided no timeline for when a transaction may be announced or completed.

The move follows its rebranding to BNB Plus from Applied DNA Sciences in November. The company still continues to  commercialize its proprietary nucleic acid production solutions for the biopharmaceutical and diagnostics markets.

It would also follow the recent high-profile pivot of Allbirds (BIRD) from trendy footwear to AI compute infrastructure. Meanwhile, a number of bitcoin-mining companies have shifted more of their focus to data center infrastructure.

data.title: BWX Technologies to buy Precision Components, bolstering nuclear manufacturing
data.description:
BWX Technologies (BWXT) said Monday it agreed to acquire Precision Components Group, a Pennsylvania-based manufacturer of custom-fabricated heavy pressure vessels, reactors, casks, and other heavy-walled components for nuclear, commercial, and government applications; financial terms were not disclosed.

The acquisition adds more than 500K sq ft of manufacturing capacity, including large-envelope machining, heavy weldments, pressure vessels, heat exchangers, and ASME-certified component fabrication, and a skilled workforce of 400-plus employees.

PCG generated ~$125M in revenues in 2025.

BWXT (BWXT) said the acquisition "represents an important first step as we establish U.S.-based commercial nuclear manufacturing capacity to meet the accelerating needs of U.S. commercial nuclear customers," said John MacQuarrie, president of BWXT Commercial Operations.

data.title: China silver imports hit record high on investor, solar demand
data.description:
China’s silver imports jumped to a record high in March, driven by strong demand from retail investors and the country’s vast solar sector, far exceeding typical seasonal levels.

The world’s biggest silver consumer imported around 836 tons last month, extending a strong run of inbound shipments so far this year, according to media reports citing Chinese customs data on Monday. That compares with a 10-year seasonal average for March of about 306 tons.

Demand was bolstered by retail investors piling into small silver bars, an alternative to pricey gold, and solar manufacturers front-loading production ahead of the removal of the export tax rebates on April 1, Bloomberg reported. The solar industry consumes about a fifth of annual supply, and is overwhelmingly located in China. Still, the high rate of imports is unlikely to endure.

Silver prices (XAGUSD:CUR) fell 1.8% to $79.34 per ounce on Monday, but up 11.5% YTD.

data.title: PepsiCo looks to broaden the market for Gatorade beyond sports participants
data.description:
PepsiCo (PEP) launched a major brand refresh for its Gatorade brand that shifts its identity from being purely a sports drink for athletes to a broader everyday hydration platform. The brand refresh includes new packaging, cleaner formulas, and products aimed at non‑athlete occasions like work, travel, and daily routines. 

Under the company's new Gatorade Advanced Hydration System, the lineup is organized by whether each drink hydrates better, faster, or longer than water, making each product’s function more obvious on the shelf. Notably for investors, the target audience for Gatorade expands from athletes to essentially anyone who needs to hydrate.

PepsiCo (PEP) highlighted that Gatorlyte Longer Lasting will begin rolling out later this year, with national availability in 2027. The new product includes a first-to-market proprietary electrolyte blend and is designed to help the body retain fluid and stay hydrated longer than water. It will be Gatorade’s highest electrolyte ready-to-drink offering and was developed for activities when maintaining hydration over extended periods matters, such as long travel days, demanding work shifts, or sustained physical activity.

Traditional sports offerings remain in place, but the portfolio now explicitly spans more occasions, pairing “hydrates longer” products for long travel or work with existing “faster” recovery drinks and “better” everyday options.

Looking back, Gatorade was created in 1965 at the University of Florida by Dr. Robert Cade’s team to rehydrate Florida Gators football players in extreme heat. Licensed to Stokely-Van Camp in 1967, it later passed to Quaker Oats in 1983 and then PepsiCo (PEP) in 2001.

data.title: Eureka Lithium taps capital markets veteran Danny Matthews as new CEO
Cybin stands out as the most undervalued psychedelic stock as Trump signs on faster PTSD research
data.description:
Psychedelic stocks surged in premarket trading on Monday after President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Saturday to dramatically accelerate research into, and expand patient access to psychedelic substances that have shown promise in treating post-traumatic stress disorder and other serious mental illnesses.

The market reaction was swift and broad. Compass Pathways (CMPS) surged 25% in premarket, while AtaiBeckley (ATAI) jumped 22%, Definium Therapeutics (DFTX) climbed 20%, and both GH Research (GHRS) and Cybin (HELP) rose 17%.

Against this backdrop of renewed policy tailwinds, the following list ranks the primary psychedelic sector stocks by their Seeking Alpha valuation grade,  a measure of how cheaply or expensively each stock is priced relative to its fundamentals. An A+ grade indicates the most undervalued, while F signals the most overvalued; a grade of B- or above is generally considered a Buy, while D+ or below is a Sell.

Cybin (HELP) stands out as the only name on the list carrying an outright value signal, earning an A grade and suggesting it may be the most attractively priced relative to fundamentals among its peers. GH Research (GHRS) and Compass Pathways (CMPS) follow with grades of C- and D+, respectively, while Definium Therapeutics (DFTX) and AtaiBeckley (ATAI) both carry D- grades, indicating that despite the premarket enthusiasm, most of these names are trading at a significant premium to their current fundamentals.

Here is the list:

  1. Cybin Inc. (HELP), Valuation grade: A

  2. GH Research PLC (GHRS), Valuation grade: C-

  3. COMPASS Pathways plc (CMPS), Valuation grade: D+

  4. Definium Therapeutics, Inc. (DFTX), Valuation grade: D-

  5. AtaiBeckley Inc. (ATAI), Valuation grade: D-
